Tests the behavior of `Math.max` when given `NaN` as an argument.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.max` function when it receives `NaN` (Not-a-Number) as an argument. The `assert` function checks if the result of `Math.max(NaN)` is `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the ECMAScript specification. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case for the `Math.max` function. It is useful for ensuring that the JavaScript environment correctly handles `NaN` inputs in mathematical operations.
